task: How many tasks do I have due next week in Tasks app? Assume the week starts from Monday. Express your answer as a single integer.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail shortcut in the dock. The "Tasks" app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app → | Step 2: Check the current date (Sunday, Oct 15) | Step 3: Navigate to tasks due next week (starting Monday, Oct 16) | Step 4: Count the tasks and return the final integer.

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Tasks app is not present on the current home screen view. To proceed with the task of checking tasks due next week, I need to launch the Tasks app first. Since it's not on the home screen, I will use the system's open app function to launch it directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The screen displays the "My Tasks" list. I can see several tasks with their due dates: "Clean Out Fridge" (Oct 8), "Take Dog to the Vet" (Oct 8), "Learn to Play Guitar" (Mon), "Research Vacation Destinations" (Mon), "Meal Prep for the Week" (Tue), "Grocery Shopping" (Wed), "Submit Expense Report" (Wed), and "Reply to Emails" (Yest). The list ends at "Due tomorrow" at the bottom, but the list is cut off, showing more tasks might be available below.

[Reflection]
The previous plan's Step 2 was to check the current date (Sunday, Oct 15) to determine next week's start (Monday, Oct 16). However, I can already see the current view covers tasks down to "Due tomorrow" (which seems to be tomorrow relative to the current day shown on the screen, likely Sunday, Oct 15). The tasks visible so far only go up to tomorrow (Yest) and some are scheduled for Mon, Tue, Wed. I need to see all tasks due next week (Mon, Oct 16 onwards) to count them accurately, but the list is currently incomplete.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Scroll down to view all tasks due next week (starting Monday) → | Step 3: Count the tasks with due dates from Monday onwards | Step 4: Return the final count.

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the current screen only shows tasks down to "Due tomorrow" (Yest, which means today is Yest/Oct 15). The tasks currently visible are either overdue or due today/tomorrow. To find all tasks due next week (starting Monday), I need to see the rest of the list. Therefore, I will swipe up to scroll down and reveal the upcoming tasks.

[Memory]
Current date assumed as Sunday, Oct 15. Tasks due next week start from Monday, Oct 16. Current visible tasks (Oct 8, Mon, Tue, Wed, Yest) are either already passed or not in the target timeframe. Need to keep scrolling to find tasks from Monday onwards.

[Observation]
The screen displays tasks grouped by their due dates: "Due tomorrow" (Complete project proposal), "Due Tuesday" (Review code changes), "Due Wednesday" (Organize files and folders), "Due Friday" (Organize movie night), "Due Saturday" (Prepare agenda for weekly meeting), and "Due Sunday, October 22" (Plan family reunion). There are no tasks due on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, or the upcoming weekend yet visible in this section.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed the week starts on Monday (Oct 16) based on a previous view, but the current screen reveals that "Due tomorrow" is Sunday, Oct 15 (since "Due Saturday" is visible). The current view shows tasks from tomorrow (Sun) to Sunday, Oct 22. I need to adjust my understanding of the timeline. The target timeframe "next week" (starting Monday) should be from Monday, Oct 16 to Sunday, Oct 22.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Scroll down to view all tasks ✓ | Step 3: Count the tasks due from Monday (Oct 16) to Sunday (Oct 22) → | Step 4: Return the final count.

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the tasks visible are due tomorrow (Sun), Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday (Oct 22). I need to identify which of these fall into the "next week" timeframe (starting Monday, Oct 16). The task "Complete project proposal" (Due tomorrow, Sunday) does not count. The tasks due on Tuesday (Review code changes), Wednesday (Organize files and folders), Friday (Organize movie night), Saturday (Prepare agenda for weekly meeting), and Sunday (Plan family reunion) all fall within the target timeframe. There are 5 tasks visible so far. However, the list might have more items below. I should scroll further down to ensure I don't miss any.

[Memory]
Current date is Sunday, Oct 15. Target timeframe: Monday, Oct 16 to Sunday, Oct 22. Counted tasks so far: 5 (Tue, Wed, Fri, Sat, Sun). Still need to check if there are more tasks below.

[Observation]
The screen shows the remaining tasks: "Review code changes" (Due Tuesday), "Organize files and folders" (Due Wednesday), "Organize movie night" (Due Friday), "Prepare agenda for weekly meeting" (Due Saturday), and "Plan family reunion" (Due Sunday, October 22). Below these, there is a "Completed" section header. The screen ends here, and no further tasks are visible.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Scroll down to view all tasks ✓ | Step 3: Count the tasks due from Monday (Oct 16) to Sunday (Oct 22) ✓ | Step 4: Return the final count →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the screen has been fully checked. The tasks due from Tuesday to Sunday (Oct 16 to Oct 22) are all visible. I have already counted 5 tasks: Tuesday (1), Wednesday (1), Friday (1), Saturday (1), and Sunday (1). Since there are no more tasks below and none are due on Monday or Thursday, the total count for next week is 5. I will now provide this final count as the answer.

[Memory]
Current date is Sunday, Oct 15. Target timeframe: Monday, Oct 16 to Sunday, Oct 22. Final count of tasks in this timeframe: 5 (Tue, Wed, Fri, Sat, Sun). No tasks due on Monday or Thursday. Task list is complete.
